author: felix314159_
content: hi, first of all thanks for trying to help secure ethereum 🙂 as far as i understand, the issues in your repo that were a problem on live mainnet have already been fixed, so there is no need for secrecy? if so, it would be best if you could clone https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs and have your agent read `.claude/commands` (a set of skills that can help you write actual test cases we can run against clients using our tooling) and then make a  PR with a minimal test for each confirmed issue you have found. if you struggle at any point feel free to ask, we actively try to make the repo more first-time-contributor friendly

author: catwith1hat
content:
<@510888004366368794> I made the repo public :). I'll see what I can get going. I was hoping to get some feedback first on: "that makes sense" or "that's all wrong" first. 

For glamsterdam, I was hoping that the devs can have a look first, because I will be slow and bal-devnet-7 is coming soon.

author: jochembrouwer
content:
I like this approach, am I correct that there are 3 bugs found in EthRex and that:
D — custom chain: category which applies to specific genesis state (e.g. reth importing 0xef01 accounts)
D — synthetic state: category which happens where we attempt to overflow certain calculates (gas limits / base fees)
D — contained upstream: most/all of these seem to be part of networking/engine API? 
mainnet-glamsterdam: these are bugs found on latest devnet?

Checking if these categories match what I suspect they are 🙂

I would suggest to also, if not yet done, integrate execution-specs in the code review. Execution-specs is used to fill most/all (?) glamsterdam tests and is thus used as source of truth for the eip implementations. If the tool finds a bug there then we should check if this is specified in the EIP, and if this is not the case then it should be clarified because then the EIP has a blind spot

author: jochembrouwer
content: Did you contact EthRex regarding the found problems? And other clients also for the related problems flagged above? If not we should redirect this repo to them 😄
